user_query:
We’re planning a firewall change on our Linux servers and I need you to coordinate the prep work and reviews. Read input/firewall_context.md for environment and change-request details, and read input/approval_graph.py (our Python-based approval workflow). Then produce these outputs:

1) Create an iptables change plan that references best practices, security considerations, performance tips, debugging guidance, migration/rollback preparation, and a quick-start checklist. Save to output/iptables_change_plan.md. Make sure the plan explicitly mentions principle of least privilege, logging, staging, rollback, and includes sections that map to intro, quickstart, patterns, debugging, performance, security, migration, and cheatsheet.

2) Review the approval workflow code and write a structured code review using these exact categories: Critical Issues, State Schema Issues, Graph Structure Issues, Async Issues, Tool Integration Issues, Checkpointing Issues, Performance Issues. Include a 10-item checklist and mark findings per category. Save to output/code_review.md.

3) Establish a network baseline: measure latency to 1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8, and 9.9.9.9, and measure both download and upload bandwidth. Output a single JSON file with timestamp and keys for latency, download, and upload. Save to output/network_test.json.

4) Prepare a message to the "netops" agent summarizing the planned firewall changes, asking them to run the baseline on their side and to acknowledge. Save only the message body to output/inter_agent_message.txt. Begin the message exactly with "[From Agent ops] " followed by the English text. Mention "netops", "firewall", "acknowledge", and reference the path output/network_test.json.

Do not print the outputs inline; write them to the specified files under output/.
